h2.name: H₂
h2.description: Dihydrogen — the canonical VQE benchmark. Two electrons in the STO-3G minimal basis map onto two qubits under Jordan–Wigner.
h2.precomputed_note: Precomputed VQE result at equilibrium bond distance. The distance slider is illustrative — energies are not re-computed live.
h2.source: Peruzzo et al., 'A variational eigenvalue solver on a photonic quantum processor,' Nature Communications 5, 4213 (2014); see also Cao et al., 'Quantum Chemistry in the Age of Quantum Computing,' Chemical Reviews 119, 10856 (2019).

hehplus.name: HeH⁺
hehplus.description: Helium hydride cation — the oldest molecule in the universe and a small two-qubit cousin of H₂ in the STO-3G basis.
hehplus.precomputed_note: Precomputed VQE result at equilibrium bond distance. The distance slider is illustrative — energies are not re-computed live.
hehplus.source: Kandala et al., 'Hardware-efficient variational quantum eigensolver for small molecules and quantum magnets,' Nature 549, 242 (2017).

lih.name: LiH
lih.description: Lithium hydride — a four-qubit problem in the minimal basis after the standard active-space reduction. Demonstrates that VQE scales beyond the textbook two-qubit case.
lih.precomputed_note: Precomputed VQE result at equilibrium bond distance. The distance slider is illustrative — energies are not re-computed live.
lih.source: Kandala et al., 'Hardware-efficient variational quantum eigensolver for small molecules and quantum magnets,' Nature 549, 242 (2017).
